item.title: Unhinged meme dump that will surely entertain you ("The 300 000 points special")
item.description:
1. Mmm, salt
2. That's one way to propose, I suppose
3. She dances better with her eyes, than me with my entire body
4. What cigarette break looks like when you're a celebrity
5. Very important tutorial, you will need this in life, trust me!
6. Ladies and gentlemen, they got him
7
8. Cookies
9
10. He shooots and he scores
11. Crowd simulation
12. Starting adult life be like
13
14. Spring time
15. Thanks to the two people (Hi, Mom and Dad!) who made it to the end of this dump. When I'm not busy making questionable life choices on the internet, I've been pouring my soul into a little passion project. If you got a kick out of this dump, you're going to love my game: "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)." Think "The Stanley Parable" but with even more chaos and quirky characters such this little nihilist pancake. Can you imagine? A first-person game mixed with humor? Anyway, please wishlist: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ It would mean the world to me and my brother if you checked out the game and gave it a wishlist. It won't disappoint. I promise. Just, whatever you do, don't press the big red button!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: Challenge Accepted :)

item.dc:creator: Luvlyquants

item.title: Yor Forger - SpyxFamily Cosplay
item.description:
The assassin version of Yor Forger from SpyxFamily! This show is still my obsession, as you can tell by my previous posts. Yor is such a fun character and I love how different of a take she is on the whole "femme fatale" assassin character. Def watch this show if you are interested! Also this cosplay was so fun to craft, despite the very tight corset lol
Character reference for y'all in case you have never seen SpyxFamily
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.title: anticapitalist dump for the 4th
item.description:
.
.
*if there's factory Work. **if there's Work for artists. ***if Work is required to live.
.
.
.
my first memory = dreaming of a carrot on a stick like this one (from the mule's pov)
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
*iirc, they're referring here to Hoggish Greedly (Captain Planet) & Murky Dismal (Rainbow Brite)...?
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
*brace yourselves for the flagrantly conspiracy-theory-positive portion of the dump*
*which isn't to say corporations aren't also organized criminals
project bluebird -> project artichoke -> mk-ultra
.
.
.
.
read david graeber if you haven't yet. [ theanarchistlibrary.org ]
abdullah ocalan also has some good stuff to say
...

item.dc:creator: NegasonicAdultEMP

item.title: Too many wallpapers on my phone dump #5
item.description:
Hey imgur. I'm asking for help, please scroll to the end. Thank you.
Hey imgur. I don't know if I am doing this right but I am in desperate need right now. My family used to consist of me (48f) my son Jason Jr. (17m), my son Johnathan (15m), and my partner Jason Sr. (45m). Both of my sons have autism and other health concerns. 5 years ago my partner fell into a diabetic coma with blood sugars at 650 at the time. He was in a coma for 3 weeks. He did come out of it but he wasn't the same person anymore. He also had severely damaged nerves in his legs and had extreme difficulty walking so was confined to a wheelchair. He had extreme difficulty with all basic functions and caring of himself. I became his primary caregiver along with being the main caregiver of our children. We had no family or friends to help. We were surviving on his disability check, barely, but were getting things taken care of. Unfortunately my partner passed away on May 30th due to complications from pneumonia and a blood infection. This devastated us. My boys were being so brave but were scared. I wasn't able to work and care for everyone at the same time and Jason Sr's needs were such that I had to take only short trips before I was needed again. And the government doesn't help with caregiver duties. But with him gone, we lost our only income. I can't even afford a cremation for Jason Sr. I have no car and haven't for years and now we have to move somehow without money because I can't afford the rent now. But that's not the worst. After a traumatic weekend and frantic ambulance ride from hell to Vanderbilt Children's Hospital in Nashville, I lost my son Jason Jr. Due to a heart infection and ataxia on June 17th. My baby's heart just stopped working. Y'all, this is so hard. My family has been severed by half. This has been so surreal and unreal. And I still don't have the money for another cremation or the money to move me and my remaining child closer to family before we're evicted. So I humbly ask for help. If you can donate or share the gofundme link that my sister set up, that would help. I honestly just need to move closer to what family I have left. Even with no car or anyone to help to actually move. I feel pathetic having to even do this but I have no choice. This is the hardest thing I have ever been through. Jason Jr. was supposed to turn 18 on July 1st. But he didn't. I don't know how to move on with this devastation in my heart. But I have to because my living son Johnathan needs me. And he deserves so much. Sorry if I didn't make any sense. Thank you for reading this. GoFundMe: https://gofund.me/de81d9cf Any help is appreciated.
item.dc:creator: Mistformlady

item.title: "Too Cruel"
item.description: Fiona Bruce photo: “Fiona Bruce, Antiques Roadshow at the Bandstand Eastbourne 23rd May 2013” By Andrew Campbell - Fiona Bruce, CC BY 2.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=90080879 https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/2.0/ Sauce: Decca Aitkenhead, “Fiona Bruce: ‘If you look like the back end of a bus, you won’t get the job.’” The Guardian. 24 August 2009 http://www.guardian.co.uk/media/2009/aug/24/decca-aitkenhead-fiona-bruce “Fiona Elizabeth Bruce (born 25 April 1964) is a British journalist, newsreader, and television presenter. She joined the BBC as a researcher for their current affairs program Panorama in 1989, and became the first female newsreader on BBC News at Ten, as well as presenting many other flagship programmes for the corporation, including BBC News at Six, Crimewatch, Real Story, Antiques Roadshow, and Fake or Fortune? Since 10 January 2019, she has been the presenter of the BBC One television programme Question Time.” https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fiona_Bruce
item.dc:creator: oukid

item.dc:creator: joeyecho101

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1063 (palm tree simulator)
item.description:
Another morning, another day experiencing the existential horrors. Let's get this bread.
Always.
It's either gonna be this or from my shitposts.
I SAW MY FIRST WANKENPANZER YESTERDAY. It was going up Schnebbly Hill Road, one of the roughest dirt roads in Northern Arizona. I wonder if it made it the full 16 miles of road to Flagstaff.
(I also saw only my second ever Rufous-crowned Sparrow in Sedona yesterday, too, but nobody cares)
Hahahahaha
This recording is indeed sad.
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "What's goin' on? Beep."
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Scorched Earth, The Mother of All Games
item.description:
I played this game so much as a kid. I lived on my dad's DOS machine after school when homework was done.
All the cool tracer lines showing your previous shots

item.dc:creator: miaormoa

item.title: Herly ferking shertberls
item.description: For over two years, he's been the loudest, horkingest, smelliest piece of shit I've ever had the displeasure to meet, and he's finally fucking leaving. Keeps his compost bin on the balcony and caused a mouse problem? That's my shitbag neighbour. Loudly BBQs at 1AM every single night? Guess fucking who. Plays cable tv constantly in the living room extra loud so he can sit out on his balcony and watch it? Oh yeah, that's him. Horks and spits loudly off his balcony? A charming and delightful fellow. Don't get me started on the coughing. It's because of the weed he smokes constantly, which I wouldn't care about of it didn't cause the horking, spitting, and coughing. Plus we live in a wood-frame building. Have I mentioned the smell? He's an older guy but it's not the smell you'd associate with an older person; it's the stench of a man who has a large dog he never, ever bathes. Oh shit! I didn't mention the dog that he yells at and calls a sissy (what?) and barely ever walks because he once told us the dog could 'hold it for over eight hours', which I'm pretty sure is questionable as hell but I'm not a dog owner. And also I think the dog just peed on the floor a lot. I haven't slept properly in over a week because he's been playing music until jesus fucking shit in the morning and I actually finally called the cops last night (which I hated doing) PLUS I actually went down to the building manager's office last week to serve them a letter of loss of quiet enjoyment. But he's gone. I think. I'm still holding my breath. I watched the truck leave while I was writing this.
item.dc:creator: ImmortalChild
